summary: Jeff Atwood humorously explores the problem of invisible trailing whitespace in code, framing it as a source of programmer anxiety. He demonstrates how code can appear perfectly fine visually while harboring hidden whitespace issues that can cause problems in source control. The post acknowledges that programmers are detail-obsessed control freaks who agonize over seemingly minor issues because small mistakes can cascade into major failures. Atwood offers several practical solutions including post-build cleanup scripts, editor macros, and whitespace visibility toggles. While conceding the world won't literally end over trailing spaces, he playfully insists on vigilance against this invisible menace.
key_points: Invisible whitespace at the end of lines represents a category of code problems you can't see but that can still cause issues
Programmers are inherently detail-obsessed because even tiny mistakes like a misplaced semicolon can break everything
Practical solutions exist: post-build cleanup scripts, editor macros, whitespace highlighting rules, and always-visible whitespace mode
The obsession with invisible details is a defining trait of the programming profession, not a personal quirk
Source control makes whitespace issues more visible and problematic when checking in changes
money_quotes: Code that is visible is code that can be wrong. No surprise there. But did you know that even the code you *can't* see may be wrong, too?
These are the questions that drive young programmers to madness.

I humbly submit that, well, you're not one of us. You don't appreciate what it's like to spend every freaking minute of every freaking day agonizing over the tiniest details of the programs you write.
Not because we *want* to, you understand, but because the world explodes when we don't.
If one semicolon is out of place, everything goes sideways. That's how programming works. It's fun! Sometimes! I swear!
We got into this industry because, quite frankly, we are control freaks. It's who we are. It's what we do.
Just because I can't see it doesn't mean it's not out to get me.
key_insight: Programming demands obsessive attention to invisible details because even the code you can't see—like trailing whitespace—can cause real problems.
